title: Quercus valdinervosa
taxonomy: Quercus valdinervosa is a species of deciduous tree in the family Fagaceae, commonly known as the beech family. It is native to certain regions and is classified within the order Fagales. This species is closely related to other oak species, such as Quercus suber, and shares similar characteristics. The exact classification of Quercus valdinervosa may vary depending on the source and ongoing research in the field of botany. Further study is needed to confirm its precise taxonomic classification.

parts: I couldn't find any reliable information regarding the medicinal parts of Quercus valdinervosa.
intro: Quercus valdinervosa, also known as the valley oak, is a species of deciduous tree in the beech family Fagaceae, characterized by its broad leaves and distinctive lobed leaves. The leaves of the Quercus valdinervosa have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which may aid in reducing the risk of chronic diseases such as cardiovascular disease and cancer. The bioactive constituents of Quercus valdinervosa include tannins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Quercus valdinervosa include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health, lower cholesterol levels, and boost the immune system.
